key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration prediction across fundamentally different welding processes without requiring new labels.
engineering_relevance: This improves intelligent welding monitoring by reducing relabeling effort and enabling robust deployment from TIG to laser welding and vice versa.
method: Train a UDA model for penetration-state classification across source and target welding domains
Use gradual source domain expansion to stabilize transfer under large process shifts
Validate feature alignment with UMAP visualization of domain-invariant, class-separable embeddings
evidence_signal: The approach achieved 90.65% and 90.72% average accuracy in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG-to-laser plus 81.13% laser-to-TIG in cross-process transfer, outperforming the supervised baseline by about 35.8% to 43.4%.
watch_item: Cross-process deployment still depends on target-process data availability during adaptation, so performance may vary if the target welding conditions differ beyond the studied datasets.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
